prevalence.source: Geriatrics epidemiology syntheses (frailty) / Brazilian aging cohort context

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Phenotype (Fried) or deficit accumulation (frailty index); screening tools in primary care (e.g., gait speed, grip strength) where used

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: limited; frailty screening not routine in APS
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; improves with exercise, nutrition, and medication optimization

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Often recognized after falls/hospitalizations; geriatric services concentrated in larger cities
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Resistance exercise, nutrition/protein optimization, deprescribing, fall prevention, and social support interventions
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited geriatric workforce and uneven rehab and social support services
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Primary care chronic disease platform can support multidomain interventions when resources exist
